grader.method: Independent 17 July 2026 course-wide semantic regrade of all 187 composed pages after concept-first visual headings, cumulative story reveals, outcome-heading removal, shared lab contrast rules, and retirement of 21 supplemental program bridges; confirmed that required transfer, depth, checks, boundaries, and provenance remain intact.

pages.rationale: The home leads with a credible full-stack promise, three concise payoffs, a five-phase and seven-frontier arc, two direct CTAs, and an honest self-contained evidence boundary. The token pipeline remains clearly illustrative rather than a measurement. E is 14 because the removed token-to-logits worked trace now belongs to the lessons; R is 4 because this home has no claim-linked source cards.

The page opens with familiar language jobs, traces a prompt and supplied meeting notes into a response without assuming human-like understanding, and teaches when current evidence, specialist judgment, or action authority is required. Its prediction-led trust-boundary explorer, changed restaurant case, objective checks, and quiz remain fully local and prerequisite-free. The only deduction is that its useful primary reading is page-level rather than pinned to the exact claims or revision discussed.

Axes, rank, broadcasting, matrix contraction, and the (2,3,4) times (4,6) to (2,3,6) trace are correct; the dependency-free notebook reproduced the stated shape. Prediction, shape debugging, objective checks, and changed-case transfer fully cover the outcomes. The source cards are direct but not section-pinned.

The shifted-softmax probabilities, cross-entropy of 2.408 for the low-probability target, and padding-aware mean are numerically correct and clearly tied to logits and targets. Learners predict, inspect failure modes, and transfer to changed distributions; references are relevant but broad rather than claim-local.

Forward values, local derivatives, chain-rule ownership, gradient accumulation, and finite-difference comparison are coherent and use correct signs and units. The PyTorch notebook was audited statically because PyTorch is not installed locally, while all required assessment remains deterministic in-browser. The only deduction is broad rather than equation-local provenance.

SGD, momentum, Adam state, learning-rate effects, and update ownership are taught through explicit numeric steps and diagnostic changed cases. The capstone adds a dependency-free executable starter, exact acceptance checks, a complete machine-readable reference, and verified provenance with no unsupported performance claim.

The page correctly separates characters, UTF-8 bytes, tokens, and IDs; the dependency-free run reproduced six characters and nine bytes. Unknown tokenizer IDs are deliberately left unmeasured rather than fabricated, and the validation dossier, retry checks, and provenance boundary make the complete teaching claim auditable.

Lookup-table shapes, token-row reuse, contextualization limits, and embedding similarity are accurately distinguished. The PyTorch example is properly environment-labeled, while the preserved dossier explicitly withholds unexecuted cosine values; objective checks and transfer diagnose row-versus-context confusions with complete provenance.

The page correctly motivates order information, contrasts absolute and rotary schemes, and demonstrates that a RoPE pair rotation preserves norm while changing relative phase; the dependency-free notebook confirmed the stated norm. Boundaries and changed-position transfer are strong, but sources are not pinned to exact equations or revisions.

Q/K/V shapes, scaled dot products, causal masking before softmax, weighted value aggregation, multi-head roles, and the grouped-query transfer are all internally consistent. The PyTorch run is dependency-labeled and the browser checks expose mask failures; the sole deduction is broad rather than claim-local source linkage.

Residual streams, attention, MLP transformation, normalization, and increasing contextual representations are connected without implying that layers have single fixed human meanings. Concrete tensor flow, intervention limits, prediction, and changed-case checks cover the objectives; references are useful but not layer-claim-local.

Input-target shifting, teacher forcing, loss masking, optimization, and the distinction between training and autoregressive generation are accurate. Token-level traces and changed masks make leakage and off-by-one errors observable, with deterministic retry paths; source cards remain page-level.

The complete tokenizer-to-block-to-logits-to-loss stack is assembled with correct shape and causal contracts, and the capstone's tiny-GPT artifact was regenerated and verified row by row. The nanochat GPT-2-grade timing is tied to the cited hardware, commit-era evidence, metric, and external-run boundary rather than presented as a local result.

Data, tokenization, batching, forward/backward/update, checkpoints, and validation are ordered causally and linked to observable artifacts rather than vague scale language. The executed token-accounting validation, explicit evidence tier, deterministic checks, and direct provenance fully support the page's outcomes.

Causal target shifting, padding and loss masks, architectural choices, and objective-dependent behavior are correctly traced with explicit tensor shapes. The PyTorch example is environment-labeled and transfer catches target leakage and mask mistakes; foundational sources are direct but not pinned to the exact implementation revision.

The 6ND compute proxy, fixed-compute trade-off, loss-curve interpretation, and stopping decisions are correctly qualified as estimates rather than universal laws; the notebook reproduced both 1.20e20-FLOP examples. Confounds and transfer are explicit, while sources are not claim-locally pinned to fit revisions.

Collection, licensing, filtering, deduplication, mixture weighting, split isolation, and contamination checks are taught as distinct causal and governance decisions. Concrete document-flow cases and diagnostic transfer prevent volume from standing in for quality; sources are primary and relevant but broad rather than claim-linked at section granularity.

Parameter, gradient, optimizer, activation, and communication memory are separated correctly, and data, tensor, pipeline, and sharded parallelism preserve distinct ownership. Ledger arithmetic, failure recovery, bottleneck diagnosis, and transfer are effective; implementation sources are current but not version-pinned throughout.

Fill-in-the-middle transformations, span choices, prefix constraints, and objective-mixture effects are described with correct input/target ownership and clear boundaries. The preserved metrics are explicitly labeled simulated rather than measured, and the validation dossier plus deterministic changed-case checks provides complete provenance.

Held-out loss, perplexity, contamination, slice evaluation, checkpoint comparison, and stopping evidence are accurately distinguished. The notebook reproduced mean loss 2.025 and perplexity 7.576, while transfer tests metric conflicts and uncertainty; references are relevant but broad rather than attached to each evaluation claim.

The OLMo 3 model-flow case correctly attributes the 5.9T-token Dolma 3 Mix, Dolmino and Longmino stages, 7B/32B releases, and reported system scale to official Ai2 evidence. Course-supplied learner-analysis rows remain labeled simulations, and the capstone supplies executable checks, a complete reference, and non-generalization boundaries.

The bridge cleanly separates base-model pretraining from supervised instruction tuning, preference learning, reinforcement learning, and safety evaluation without collapsing their objectives. Its validation artifact states which comparisons are published release evidence versus course interpretation, with exact retry and transfer checks and direct provenance.

SFT, preference optimization, RL, tool/safety tuning, and evaluation are placed in a coherent but non-universal recipe with stage-specific data and evidence. The Tülu release ledger is accurately reproduced from primary records, distinguishes reported metrics from inference, and is paired with diagnostic local checks.

Chat formatting, assistant-only loss, masking, packing, QLoRA configuration, baseline comparison, and artifact packaging are accurate and current. The optional GPU workshop gives hardware profiles, a readiness gate, bounded stages, expected observations, stop criteria, and a pinned model ID; its package installation remains environment-current rather than fully lockfile-pinned, earning the provenance deduction.

Chosen/rejected pairs, reference-policy control, DPO score differences, length effects, and preference-data limitations are causally explained rather than reduced to a slogan. Worked pair comparisons and transfer expose confounding and label quality; the foundational sources are direct but not pinned to exact implementation revisions.

States, actions, trajectories, rewards, returns, policies, baselines, and credit assignment are introduced in plain language and then connected to sequence generation. Numeric return and advantage traces, reward-hacking boundaries, and changed-case checks are coherent; references are broad rather than equation-local.

Preference collection, reward-model fitting, policy updates, KL control, evaluation, and failure modes are correctly separated, including what the reward model does not prove. Learners diagnose reward exploitation and distribution shift through committed changed cases; sources are primary but page-level rather than claim-local.

Tool schemas, argument validation, execution authority, least privilege, refusal behavior, and safety evaluation are assigned to the correct system owners. Concrete call traces and injection/failure transfer preserve the distinction between model output and authorized action; official sources are useful but not version-pinned throughout.

The Tülu 3 SFT to length-normalized DPO to RLVR sequence, 3.1 stage metrics, and DR Tulu's Qwen3/MCP/RLER extension agree with official Ai2 and model-card evidence. Every number is provenance-linked, confounds are named, and the capstone supplies a filled reference fixture that explicitly requires reproduction before making a new measurement claim.

Greedy, temperature, top-k, top-p, and stochastic sampling are correctly separated, including ordering and renormalization. The notebook reproduced retained tokens [0,1,2] and probabilities .5556/.2778/.1667, while transfer diagnoses support and randomness changes; sources remain broad.

Prefill versus decode, per-layer key/value ownership, cache growth, grouped-query dimensions, and invalidation conditions are correct. The 32-layer, 8-KV-head, 128-dim, 8192-token, bf16 ledger correctly yields 1.0 GiB and the notebook confirms it; provenance is not claim-locally versioned.

Scale, rounding, clipping, reconstruction error, weight-versus-activation choices, and memory accounting are correct. The signed three-bit symmetric example explicitly accounts for its unused code and the notebook reproduced every quantized value and error; source cards are direct but not tied to exact library revisions.

Batching, admission, prefill/decode scheduling, latency components, throughput, goodput, and overload trade-offs are correctly distinguished. The dependency-free fixture confirms throughput 2 and goodput 1, and changed SLO cases prevent throughput-only reasoning; vLLM evidence is current but the reading set is not uniformly version-pinned.

Sampling budgets, candidate diversity, verifiers, aggregation, coverage, latency, and stopping rules are taught without treating more compute as guaranteed accuracy. The scheduling artifact is verified from every request row and explicitly labeled a deterministic fixture rather than a model-quality or hardware measurement; the capstone has complete evidence and retry contracts.

Instruction hierarchy, context placement, examples, delimiters, token budgets, and context conflicts are accurately connected to model inputs rather than magical prompt wording. Concrete prompt transformations and injection-aware transfer are diagnostic; official sources are relevant but not version-pinned at the individual claim level.

Chunking, embedding, similarity search, filtering, reranking, grounded generation, and retrieval evaluation are sequenced correctly. Hand-authored similarity values are visibly labeled toy evidence, and changed-query checks diagnose retrieval versus generation failures; the reading is broad rather than tied to exact index/model revisions.

Observe-plan-act-check loops, tool contracts, state, stopping, authorization, retries, and failure containment are assigned to explicit owners. Scenario traces require learners to separate a proposed call from an executed action and diagnose loops; sources are current but not uniformly pinned to tool-interface revisions.

Construct definition, sampling, rubrics, deterministic metrics, human judgment, LLM judges, calibration, uncertainty, and leakage are accurately separated. The deterministic teaching fixture is explicitly invented and transfer requires adjudicating disagreements, while sources are direct but not claim-locally versioned.

Prompt injection, data exfiltration, privilege boundaries, secrets, logging, privacy, sandboxing, and defense in depth are correctly treated as system risks rather than prompt-only problems. Adversarial scenarios and retry feedback are concrete; standards and official guidance are useful but not revision-pinned throughout.

Trace lineage, latency and token metrics, cost attribution, quality/safety signals, retention, release gates, and rollback ownership are taught with current vLLM and OpenTelemetry semantics. The safe-agent reference artifact is machine-readable, verified, and explicitly bounded as a fixture requiring environment replay and approval for production claims.

Teacher targets, hard and soft labels, temperature, KL-style matching, data coverage, capacity gaps, and evaluation are accurately distinguished. Numeric distribution comparisons and changed-domain transfer expose when imitation fails; primary sources are relevant but not tied to exact equations or implementation revisions.

The low-rank update, parameter-count arithmetic, frozen-base ownership, merge behavior, and LoRA-versus-QLoRA distinction are correct. The common A-nonzero/B-zero initialization is explained precisely: B receives the first gradient while A initially receives zero, and both-zero would block both; the only deduction is unpinned library documentation.

Router scores, top-k dispatch, expert computation, weighted combination, capacity, overflow, load balance, and total-versus-active parameters are coherent. The 1024/8 times 1.25 capacity of 160 and 50-token overflow case are correct and diagnostic; source cards are broad rather than implementation-version-specific.

Vision encoding, patch tokens, projection into the language space, fusion, alignment, grounding, and modality-specific failure modes are accurately presented. The 224/16 calculation yields a 14 by 14 grid and 196 patches, or 197 with a class token; hand-authored highlights are labeled, while sources are not revision-pinned throughout.

Probes, attribution, activation intervention, causal evidence, specificity, edit efficacy, side effects, and falsification are carefully distinguished. The artifact verifier recomputed summaries from every raw fixture row, and the page explicitly states that these are not real GPT-2 activations and that edit efficacy remains unmeasured, preserving complete provenance.
